methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Candela is a Series C-stage transportation company that has raised €129M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises seven private peers in the transportation sector, including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft. No public comparables exist in this narrow subsector. The scarcity of same-stage and adjacent-stage peers with similar valuation profiles constrains traditional multiples-based analysis.

The peer-median approach is not applicable here. The transportation sector yields only two same-stage peers and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier comparables, falling below the three-datapoint threshold required for robust median triangulation. Expanding to all seven transportation peers would introduce stage and scale heterogeneity that would distort any derived multiple.

Without sufficient narrow-set peer density, this valuation requires alternative approaches not provided in the available data—such as discounted cash flow analysis, venture capital method application, or transaction precedent review. The key uncertainty is the absence of a sufficiently dense and homogeneous comp set. Any valuation conclusion drawn from this limited dataset should be treated as preliminary pending additional financial modeling or market data. The stage (Series C) and capital deployed (€129M+) suggest meaningful product-market validation, but that observation alone cannot substitute for quantitative benchmarking.
